miss_root_cause: Original ask was unusually precise (exact classes + reference component + cancellation check pattern), so implementation had little room to drift.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: n/a — shipped clean on first attempt; reviewer raised no concerns.
proposed_workflow_change: When tester cannot exercise a conditional branch pre-merge (e.g. no cancelled team in DB), require post-merge impersonation verification to be scheduled, not assumed.
notes: Tester verified active state pre-merge but cancelled state only post-merge via impersonation (~1 min after merge). No defect, but the conditional branch shipped without visual verification. PR #952 merged with zero reviewer comments. Minor: diff churn (86 deletions) is formatting shift from adding conditional, not real change.

miss_root_cause: Architect declared 'no code changes needed' and proposed a Firestore data-drift investigation, but the real cause was three independent code bugs (auth Response handling, client array vs string param, missing teamId filter on learner courses query).
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Architect should have read the unassignment handler + client call site + learner-courses loader to form a falsifiable code-vs-data hypothesis before concluding the issue was data-only.
proposed_workflow_change: Architect must trace the failing request end-to-end (client → API route → query → render) and cite code paths before declaring a bug is data-only; 'no code changes needed' requires evidence, not assumption.
notes: Tester caught two distinct misses across two rounds: 200-OK-but-no-delete (client array vs string), then UI still showing unassigned completed courses (missing teamId filter). 4/7 matrix scenarios unverifiable b/c they required production MS Plus credentials. Copilot left 4 non-blocking PR comments addressed in b26daa9.

miss_root_cause: Intent Analyst and Refiner read the empty original description as 'unassign all learners' (bulk/global), but the actual ask was single-learner unassignment from the global courses dashboard; human rejection closed PR #982 and forced a full re-implementation.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Intent Analyst should have flagged the empty original description as an ambiguity requiring human clarification instead of inventing a bulk-unassign interpretation from the title alone.
proposed_workflow_change: When original-description.md is empty or near-empty, Intent Analyst must escalate to human for clarification before proceeding past Phase 1 rather than fabricating success signals from the title.
notes: Empty original-description.md → Intent fabricated 'Unassign All Learners' scope. Two in-flight failures (TypeError fetcher, Firestore 500-op batch) fixed before human-rejected PR #982. Rework PR #983 merged clean. Reviewer also asked to remove .claude/ from PR — committed by mistake.

miss_root_cause: Intent and Plan treated 'group by room on mobile' per concurrent time-slot group, but reviewer wanted ONE continuous room block spanning all concurrent slots between breaks.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Intent Analyst should have asked for a concrete mobile mock or example showing the boundary rule (what defines a 'block') before Architect locked the data shape.
proposed_workflow_change: When intent mentions 'group by X on mobile', Intent Analyst must explicitly state the group boundary rule (per slot? between breaks? whole day?) and Architect must encode it in the test matrix as a concrete count assertion.
notes: First PR #989 closed after reviewer flagged 7 mobile issues (room grouping, day width, MapPin, sticky menu, expand icon, padding, room order). Sticky menu lived outside eventify-program.tsx — scope-mapping miss. Round-1 Tester caught 4/7; Round-2 7/7 pass. Code review noted redundant inner while loop kept for safety.

miss_root_cause: Shipped clean; Tester 1/1 pass, no human review comments, PR merged same day.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: N/A — no miss surfaced to human reviewers.
proposed_workflow_change: Architect should verify route paths in test matrix against the actual app router before handoff (matrix used /subscription/on-demand/register which does not exist; Tester adapted by injecting modal HTML).
notes: Trivial fix: replaced ' with ' in invoice-success-modal.tsx (2 lines) + prettier fix in unrelated eventify-program.tsx. Internal sub-agent friction only: (1) test matrix used non-existent route, Tester adapted via injected HTML; (2) Code-review round 1 blocked on missing TDD-skip rationale in plan.md (doc-only fix, no code change). Project-wide test infra broken (@jest/globals missing) drove TDD skip.

miss_root_cause: The tooltip-accessibility fix added [&:disabled]:pointer-events-auto so hover would fire, but LearnersInviteDialog wraps Button in DropdownMenu — pointer events now reached the disabled trigger and the dropdown still opened, exposing Invite via Email and Bulk Upload to team-admins on cancelled teams.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Tester should have clicked every disabled button (not just hovered for tooltip) and Architect's matrix should have listed 'disabled control exposes no further interaction' as an explicit scenario for dropdown-wrapped buttons.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls: 'Disabled buttons inside DropdownMenu/Popover triggers must conditionally unmount the wrapper when disabled — pointer-events-auto re-enables click-through and bypasses the disabled prop.'
notes: Intent + matrix listed wrong route (/dashboard/teams) for AC1 — actual route /dashboard/learners; caught at Tester round 1. Three Tester rounds wasted on stale dev server cache (PID 99066 served pre-fix code); restart fixed it. Super-admin bypass missed on 4 buttons across rounds; one more found in code review. Tooltip pointer-events-none vs Radix Tooltip conflict drove Round 4 rework. Human reviewer caught dropdown-still-opens post-PR. lines_added/deleted dominated by pnpm-lock.yaml + Learners dialog reformat.

miss_root_cause: Developer updated the Storyblok story via the Management API but never published it, so the live mobile nav (which reads the published version) still showed old data.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A publish step plus a published-state check in the Developer's Storyblok workflow before handing off to QA.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md -> Storyblok: 'After any Management API content change, publish the story and verify the published version before QA -- the live site reads published, not drafts.'
notes: Empty-commit CMS-only change; PR #993 closed unmerged ('CMS change only, closing') -- expected, not a failure. Human: 'Great thanks'. Tester correctly caught the unpublished story (1 round-trip). CI fails unrelated (empty PR). Test matrix had 2 doc errors: 768px expected mobile nav (app shows desktop), phantom 'Community' entry. tester_failures=4 approx; workpad '0/5' phrasing ambiguous.

miss_root_cause: The Intent Analyst read the original ask's 'incremental backups on content changes' as a manual step and explicitly put automation out of scope, so the first build shipped CLI scripts instead of the event-triggered webhook the user actually wanted.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Treating 'on content changes' as an automatic-trigger requirement, or asking the user whether backups should fire on their own before building, would have surfaced the webhook need before the first delivery.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md -> Intent: phrases like 'on content changes' or 'on X' signal an automatic trigger (webhook/cron/event), not a manual step -- confirm the trigger mechanism before scoping automation out.
notes: First delivery (PR #974) shipped manual CLI scripts and passed Tester 8/8, but Silas rejected it for a Firebase webhook auto-triggering incremental backups to a dedicated repo (rework=PR#977). Review caught 3 real bugs (SHA1 not SHA256, action prefix, redundant push). 9/13 scenarios blocked on org-admin/Firebase access. The one rework cycle was human-driven.

miss_root_cause: Plan assumed Vitest but project uses Jest; developer caught it and used Jest correctly.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Architect should inspect package.json for existing test runner before proposing one.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Architect checklist: 'Before proposing a test runner, framework, or tool, grep package.json and lockfile for what is already installed — do not assume.'
notes: Plan drifted to Vitest while project uses Jest; reviewer flagged as non-blocking suggestion. 3 CI follow-up commits fixed pnpm version mismatch w/ workflow before final green. No Tester or human rework.

miss_root_cause: Intent brief framed the ask as workflow-UI summary only; user actually wanted the coverage shown inline on the PR, which only surfaced after first delivery.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Intent Analyst should have asked where the report should render (workflow logs vs PR comment vs file annotations) since the original ask 'report including test coverage' is surface-ambiguous.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Intent gotchas: for CI/reporting tickets, always ask the user which surface the report should appear on (workflow logs, PR comment, or GitHub Check annotations) — each requires a different action and cannot be swapped late.
notes: First delivery shipped workflow-summary + artifact only; user pushed back wanting inline PR coverage. Three actions tried (shravan097 hung, ArtiomTr 403 on checks:write, romeovs landed). Test matrix row #4 added a requirement not in intent.md — architect drift. Phase 5 also skipped first time: PR opened but no Linear comment / In Review flip until rework.

miss_root_cause: All five Tester scenarios were unrunnable: app points at prod DB, no Firestore emulator, no invite fixtures, and the new Jest test could not execute due to a repo-wide csv-parse ESM config issue.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Architect recognising this was a backend-only defensive fix and planning a unit/integration matrix (Firestore emulator or mocked doc ref) instead of E2E scenarios that require malformed prod data.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Architect: 'For server-only validation fixes, prefer a unit/integration test matrix (emulator or mocked Firestore) over E2E scenarios; flag the Jest csv-parse ESM gap so server-side tests can run.'
notes: Backend-only fix: empty-string groupId guard in assignUserToGroup + invite route. Code review approved, CI green, merged in PR #1010. Tester recorded 0/5 due to env constraints, not defects, and Jest ESM blocks the new test from running — both real workflow gaps even though the ticket shipped clean.

primary_miss: chose silent fail over loud fail
miss_root_cause: Intent Brief resolved the silent-vs-loud failure ambiguity by assuming log+early-return so Stripe marks the webhook delivered, but project convention (per human reviewer) is to throw so ops get alerted.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Intent Analyst should have surfaced the fail-mode ambiguity to the human or grepped existing webhook handlers for the prevailing pattern instead of guessing.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Stripe webhooks: validation failures must logger.error + throw (loud failure to trigger ops alert), never silently log + return early — webhook should be retried/surfaced, not marked delivered.
notes: Original ticket = 1 file (subscriptions.ts). Human PR review expanded scope to all webhook handlers + flipped fail-mode (log→throw). 2nd review caught notification-before-validation bug in handleSubscriptionDeleted (cancellation email sent on bad data). 3 commits. No Tester failures; all caught by humans + code-review agent.

miss_root_cause: Intent and plan framed the bug as a Cloudflare redirect only; ignored Algolia indexing and relative-link rendering that depended on the same URL path.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Architect should have traced every system that references /courses/* (search indexer, link normalisation) before declaring the fix scope.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls → 'When a URL path changes or is preserved against a redirect, audit search indexer (handleStoryblokUpdate dataType branching) and Storyblok cached_url consumers for relative-path regressions.'
notes: Initial Phase 5 shipped only a smoke-test PR (#996) blocked on Cloudflare config. After human applied Cloudflare rule, reviewer flagged Algolia + relative-link bugs; rework PR #1006 fixed both plus a Tester-caught malformed-href in normalizeStoryblokUrl.

miss_root_cause: Verification compared full-page screenshots at 1280px but never clicked the course/pathway links or FAQ accordions, so a broken link wiring went undetected and merged.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: An interaction smoke test after the refactor — click each course link, pathway CTA, and FAQ row — would have caught the dead links before review.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls → 'Component-extraction refactors: visual parity is not enough — click every link, button, and toggle in the changed sections before declaring done.'
notes: State trail Todo→Dev→Review→Human Dev→Ready→Done. Linear comment from author after merge: 'Links don't work yet. Manual intervention needed.' PR had zero review comments — the miss was caught only after merge in prod-like check.

miss_root_cause: Architect and Developer fixed routes/$.tsx catchall, but /events/* is handled by the more specific events.$slug._index.tsx loader, which hardcoded version: 'published' and ignored the fix.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Resolving the user-supplied test URL against the actual route table (Remix file-based routing precedence) before planning would have surfaced events.$slug._index.tsx as the real loader.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls → Remix routing: 'When the ticket gives a concrete test URL, Architect must map it to the most-specific matching route file before writing the plan — a catchall fix will not run if a more-specific route exists.'
notes: Three attempts: PR #1007 closed (failing CI), v2 rework fixed test infra and removed wrong region param, v3 finally traced /events/* to events.$slug._index.tsx and threaded version through getEventBySlug. Tester blocked on CMS auth for scenarios 1-2 across all rounds, so functional miss only caught by human.

primary_miss: no demo story, event page untested
miss_root_cause: Plan declared 'no code changes required' after seeing existing EmbedVideo component, so neither a CMS demo story nor the event-page render path was exercised before delivery.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Test matrix should require an actual Storyblok story created via Management API and verification on every consumer route (regular pages and event pages), not just type-schema checks.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Storyblok: 'Block tickets must create a demo story via Management API and verify rendering on every consumer route — event pages use useContent defaultBlokResolver and need explicit blok handlers separate from regular pages.'
notes: Two reviewer rejections: PR#1009 closed b/c no Storyblok story shown, then event-page render path missed (useContent defaultBlokResolver lacked embed_video handler). Most of diff is pre-existing prettier/typecheck cleanup, not feature code. Code Reviewer caught optional-chaining bug on first round.

miss_root_cause: Symphony merged code + docs on 2026-05-13 but the AC required production deploy + Storyblok republish + Cloudflare exclusion, which only a human could do; ticket sat 19 days until user moved to Done 'optimistically'.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Delivery comment should have flagged the unverified AC checkbox and parked the ticket in 'Waiting on deploy/ops' rather than 'Ready for review', so the human action items were not lost.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls → 'When AC needs prod deploy, Storyblok republish, or Cloudflare/ops action, Delivery must list those handoff steps explicitly and Symphony should not treat the ticket as shippable until they are scheduled or done.'
notes: Original ask said 'investigate, do not change anything, write manual config instructions'. Refined scope added a code change (11-line *_landing override in handleStoryblokUpdate.ts) + two docs. User accepted but had to drive deploy, republish, and Cloudflare rule themselves; no PR review comments, no QA artefacts in .claude/.

miss_root_cause: Developer used `import from 'vitest'` in packages/app/src/lib/team.test.ts but that package runs Jest with @jest/globals; CI broke the build and reviewer had to flag failing tests.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Actually running the test suite in the VERIFY gate instead of relying on typecheck+lint alone (the verify-changes.sh script silently no-op'd on bash 3.2 due to a mapfile compat bug).
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Testing: 'packages/app uses Jest — import test helpers from @jest/globals (see storyblokUrl.test.ts), never vitest'; also fix verify-changes.sh bash 3.2 compat or fail loud when the script cannot run so VERIFY does not silently pass.
notes: Tester could not run E2E (no Stripe test mode, no test cancelled-team user, no Firebase write access) so accepted via code review + 11 unit tests. Reasonable given constraints but the suite-not-run gap is the same gap that hid the bad import. Fix: real test execution in VERIFY + test-account/emulator setup as separate infra ticket.

primary_miss: no PR, no Tester, no review
miss_root_cause: Symphony stopped after Phase 2 (Architect); commits landed on branch but no PR was opened and the ticket was moved straight from Dev in Progress to Done, skipping Tester, Code Review, and In Review.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A gate that refuses to mark a ticket Done unless a merged PR exists and Phases 3–5 in .claude/workpad.md are checked.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Workflow rules: 'Do not move a Linear ticket to Done until the PR is merged and .claude/workpad.md Phases 3–5 are checked; if pipeline is intentionally abbreviated for a trivial fix, record the reason in workpad before state change.'
notes: Follow-up to commit e159f3a which made timestamp fields nullable but missed notifiedAt. Diagnosed from prod stack trace in getUserCourseAssignments (Linear comment). 4 commits on branch feature/tea-4227-fix-training-dashboard-schema, no PR opened, state set to Done by Silas Palmer. Workpad Phases 3–5 still unchecked.

miss_root_cause: Architect wrote test matrix expectations from the original ask without verifying actual Eventify API data, so scenario 2 expected a fuller title than Eventify returns and scenarios 3-4 had no matching speakers.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Architect should sample real Eventify speaker data when drafting the test matrix for any ticket whose ACs depend on external API field contents.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md - Architect: when ACs reference external-API field values, sample live data (or fixture) before writing test matrix expectations.
notes: Code fix was small (formatSpeakerRole combines Position+Company). Implementation passed code review and unit tests. VERIFY gate had 31 pre-existing TS errors (jest-dom types) filed as TEA-4225; not caused by this change. No human PR comments; no dev round-trip. Rework was test-matrix reconciliation, not code.

miss_root_cause: Developer relaxed price validation to allow 0 but missed display-side `event.price &&` falsy check in EventCard that would hide the price block for $0.00 events; code reviewer caught it on round 1.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A repo-wide grep for `event.price &&` and ternary truthiness checks on `price` as part of the Architect plan when relaxing a numeric validator to allow 0.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls → 'When relaxing a numeric validator to allow 0, grep all display sites for falsy checks (`field &&`, `field ?`) on that field and replace with `typeof field === "number"`.'
notes: Tester fully blocked by Vite dep-cache corruption + bash/zsh `mapfile` incompat in verify-changes.sh — no scenarios run, no screenshots. Code review (2 rounds) substituted. EventDates.tsx still shows $0.00 instead of 'Free' (non-blocking suggestion left unfixed). PR #1029 open at retro time.

miss_root_cause: No miss — SSR/hydration root cause identified in plan, fix matched, all matrix rows passed first run, code review approved with no blocking findings, PR merged with no human review comments.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: N/A — pipeline ran end-to-end without intervention.
notes: Bug fix w/ regression test added (countdown.test.tsx +86). A11y audit surfaced 6 pre-existing banner/page issues — correctly scoped out of ticket and recommended for separate ticket. One minor non-blocking suggestion in code review (empty catch block) left as-is.

miss_root_cause: Developer added purchaseType branching to the checkout branch of RegistrationSuccessModal but left the isInvoice branch hardcoded to membership text, so event invoices still showed membership activation copy.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Plan task 3 only listed checkout_success body text changes; an explicit subtask for the isInvoice branch (and a matching plan→matrix cross-check) would have surfaced the gap before implementation.
proposed_workflow_change: Architect must enumerate every conditional branch the test matrix asserts on (checkout vs invoice × each purchaseType) as explicit plan subtasks so Developer cannot miss a branch.
notes: Tester caught the event-invoice miss (1 cycle, fixed in a721509). A11y audit then found 4 ARIA gaps (aria-modal, Close aria-label, decorative emoji, DialogDescription) fixed in c42a942 — a shared dialog primitive issue, broader than this ticket. Code review raised 3 blocking items, all resolved. PR #1026 merged, CI green, no human reviewer comments.

miss_root_cause: No miss; one-line condition change shipped cleanly with full test coverage and APPROVE review.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: N/A — shipped clean on first Tester pass and first code review.
proposed_workflow_change: Pre-existing typecheck/lint errors in unrelated packages blocked VERIFY and forced 4 extra cleanup commits on a 1-line feature; add a Phase 0 baseline VERIFY check so unrelated tech debt is fixed in a separate PR before feature work starts.
notes: Core feature was 1-line condition change (event.price && → event.price > 0 &&) in EventCard.tsx. PR ballooned to 14 files b/c pre-existing typecheck errors in app+functions blocked VERIFY, requiring redis/gaxios/lodash type fixes. Tester 3/3 pass first run. Code review APPROVE, LOW risk.

miss_root_cause: Symphony guessed StateSelector .length check was the cause from a screenshot alone; no reproduction was attempted before coding a fix.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: An Intent/Architect gate that requires a reproducible failure (in-app, devtools console, or PostHog session replay) before any code change is planned.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Bug intake: require a verified local or PostHog reproduction before Architect plans a fix; if not reproducible, route to a human-triage comment instead of Dev in Progress.
notes: Workpad stopped at Phase 2; no PR opened, no Tester/Review/Delivery. Human (Wei Lin) investigated, could not reproduce on prod or PostHog, closed Dev-in-Progress → Done as a likely local Chrome cache/extension issue on the reporter's profile. Symphony's speculative fix sits unmerged on feature/tea-4237-timezone-picker-fix.

miss_root_cause: Architect locked an in-place mutation of course_assignments without mapping SCORM registrationId continuity, cert FK preservation, or rollback path; human reworked to parallel-collection design (3 new collections, deterministic IDs).
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Architect step should enumerate downstream FKs, external system IDs, and rollback strategy before locking schema mutations to live collections.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls → 'Plans that mutate a live Firestore collection must list downstream FKs (e.g. certificates.courseAssignmentId), external system IDs (e.g. SCORM registrationId), and rollback path before lock.'
notes: Symphony stopped at Phase 3/8 (6/16 tasks per workpad). No Tester, no review, no Delivery. Human posted detailed parallel-impl rework w/ deterministic IDs + drift watermark. PR #985 still OPEN; Linear moved to Done manually after human took over the design.

miss_root_cause: Developer keyed merged list by courseId, which is not unique once current+history assignments are unioned, so React deduped rows and dropped data.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Developer self-review checklist for list keys when merging collections, or a loader test asserting same courseId appearing in both collections renders both rows.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls → React: 'When rendering a list merged from multiple Firestore collections, key by document id (assignment.id), never by foreign key (courseId) — duplicates will collide and drop rows during hydration.'
notes: Code Reviewer caught hydration key bug after first impl (commit 56ddf10 fixed). Tester verified only 1/10 scenarios — 9 blocked by missing prod test data; loader-level test was planned in plan.md but skipped citing no existing route test pattern. Merged clean, no human PR comments.

miss_root_cause: Plan claimed existing badge display was correct and declared display logic out-of-scope without reading lines 221-228; the badge only rendered after period end, missing the active-but-cancelled state that Tester later caught against AC2/AC3.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Architect verifying the cited badge implementation by quoting the lines and reconciling against the test matrix, which had already scoped AC2/AC3 into testing — the plan↔matrix contradiction was the smoking gun.
proposed_workflow_change: Architect must not declare an existing implementation correct without quoting the relevant lines and reconciling against the test matrix; any plan-OOS item still listed in the matrix is a blocking contradiction to resolve before handoff.
notes: PR carried heavy whitespace churn (tabs↔spaces) across team.test.ts and dashboard.billing.tsx due to pre-existing 128K+ lint errors on main; modified-files prettier-check forced wide reformat, hurting review-friendliness. Developer also shipped a typo (resovledActiveDateString) that code review caught. Closed as duplicated by TEA-4255.

miss_root_cause: Developer built a new BrandRainbow visual primitive without searching for the existing HeroColourStripe component that already covered the same design.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A pre-implementation grep of packages/app/src/components for existing visual primitives before scaffolding new ones.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls → 'Before adding a small visual primitive (stripes, dividers, brand bars), grep components/ for similar wrappers (e.g. HeroColourStripe) and reuse instead of duplicating.'
notes: No .claude/ symphony artefacts present (intent, workpad, qa-results all absent) so rework count is inferred from git history. Automated code review flagged blocking race condition + missing env validation; both fixed in follow-up commits before merge. Human reviewer raised one DRY suggestion (HeroColourStripe reuse), then approved.

miss_root_cause: Architect planned only toggle-time invitation creation; existing groups with selfSignUp:true but no invitationId stayed broken until Tester round 5 caught it.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Architect inspecting actual Firestore state of the two groups named in the ticket before scoping the fix.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Architect: 'When a ticket names specific affected records, the plan must include a data-state check on those records before scoping fix vs. backfill.'
notes: Code review caught 3 race/transaction issues in the auto-repair path added during rework. Round-3 false failure traced to dev-server hot-reload not picking up 991e0d5; only resolved after a clean restart. TDD skipped per workpad (route-action test env complex).

miss_root_cause: First attempt shipped without lucide icons (a stated AC), and the rework spent six tester retries chasing a cache/serialization symptom before identifying that root.tsx loader was overriding root.server.tsx — the real fix was porting the super-admin branch into root.tsx.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: An Architect-level check of which loader file actually runs (React Router v7 root.tsx > root.server.tsx) and a Tester scenario explicitly asserting an icon node next to every menu item on round 1.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls → React Router v7: 'When both root.tsx and root.server.tsx exist, root.tsx loader takes precedence; treat root.server.tsx as dead unless you delete the root.tsx loader. Never duplicate loader logic across both.'
notes: Two PR attempts (#972 then #1013). First merged then reopened by reviewer for missing lucide icons. Rework hit 6 tester failures on scenario 1 — false root causes (serialization, cache contamination) before real one (root.tsx loader shadowing root.server.tsx). Scenario 6 admin regression never E2E-verified due to login selector issues.

miss_root_cause: First commit left `hasBillingContactActions` keyed on the removed `canCancelMembership`, hiding the new button in some states; code review round 1 caught it, not VERIFY or Tester.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A grep for all references to `canCancelMembership` before deleting it, or a passing E2E run on the test matrix.
proposed_workflow_change: Developer self-review checklist should require grepping for every removed/renamed identifier across the changed file before commit, especially boolean gates used by multiple sections.
notes: Tester blocked by Vite 504 + login API not firing (env). VERIFY blocked by ~128k pre-existing prettier violations in app pkg. Shipped on code-review approval w/o screenshot or E2E. Two unrelated env blockers in one ticket — Symphony has no fallback when both VERIFY and Tester are blocked.

miss_root_cause: Initial fix swallowed all getInvoice errors as 'missing invoice'; code-review caught that auth/timeout/rate-limit failures would silently create duplicate Xero invoices.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A unit test for payXeroInvoice error paths (or a developer-side checklist for narrowing catch blocks to specific error codes) would have caught the broad handler before code-review.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls: 'catch blocks around external API existence checks must narrow on HTTP 404 (or equivalent), never treat all errors as not-found — propagate auth/timeout/5xx'.
notes: Tester Phase 4 E2E deferred: no Xero creds, live Stripe key only, no staging. Behavioural coverage relied on code-review + post-deploy manual. functions package has no jest/vitest infra — test gap that masked the catch-all handler bug until code-review.

miss_root_cause: Tester confirmed the Firestore catch-all read rule was removed but did not test that legitimate authenticated reads (events, users, event_tickets) still worked, and missed three console.error siblings of the one fixed file.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Test matrix should require positive-path coverage for every read previously served by a removed rule, and a repo-wide grep for the anti-pattern being fixed.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Security patches: when removing or tightening a Firestore rule, the test matrix must list a positive read scenario for every collection that depended on it; when fixing a logging anti-pattern, grep the whole package for siblings before declaring done.
notes: Code Review caught 4 blocking issues Tester passed: catch-all removal broke client reads, plus 3 console.error in firebase/event/index.ts. Phase 5 resume on 2026-06-17 also needed a logger object export fix (15891f61) — typecheck regression VERIFY waved through as 'pre-existing'.

miss_root_cause: Intent Analyst converted a design/discovery brief (wireframes, JTBD, before-after flows) into implementation ACs and Symphony started building the dashboard.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Classifying tickets whose stated outputs are wireframes/proposals/JTBD as design work and pausing for human confirmation before refining into build ACs.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Intent Analyst: 'If original description names wireframes, JTBD, proposal, or before-after flows as outputs, classify as design ticket and stop before refining into implementation ACs.'
notes: Human moved ticket to Done with comment 'Design ticket, accidently mangled by Claude.' Symphony reached Phase 3 (6 commits on branch, no PR opened, no QA run, no review). Workpad still shows Phase 3 unchecked. Linear had design label history (Design in Progress → Dev in Progress → Done).

miss_root_cause: Developer spent ~11 attempts fixing 197 unrelated typecheck errors before applying VERIFY.md's pre-existing-failure exception (capture evidence, file Backlog ticket, continue).
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: On first VERIFY fail, check whether failing files are in the diff; if none, immediately apply VERIFY.md exception path instead of attempting package-wide fixes.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls: 'When VERIFY fails only on files outside your diff, do NOT attempt repo-wide fixes — follow VERIFY.md exception (confirm on origin/main, file Backlog ticket, continue).'
notes: Real change ~22 lines in dashboard.tsx; rest is prettier-noise + 17 pre-existing typecheck fixes added during VERIFY loop. Tester also caught data-tour attr not rendering on NavLink (b82af96 moved attr to inner div). No human PR review comments; only bot coverage.

miss_root_cause: No miss — investigation revealed pdf.ts already used completedAt correctly; the real fix was a regeneration script for stale historical certificates.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Earlier diff-vs-claim check would have flagged that commit message 'use completedAt for cert dates' implies a code change that the diff does not contain (no pdf.ts edit).
proposed_workflow_change: Developer/Code Reviewer should verify commit message verbs match actual diff scope — a 'fix' commit with no production code change should rephrase to 'add regeneration script' or similar.
notes: Package-wide functions:lint failed on 14k pre-existing prettier errors; filed TEA-4295 and shipped w/ documented caveat. E2E partial: 1/4 scenarios verified via UI, 3/4 blocked by test data (identical assignedAt/completedAt). All scenarios passed code review. No human review comments on PR #1071.

miss_root_cause: Backend webhook flow requires Klaviyo and Stripe dashboard access; the browser-automation Tester cannot reach either, and the functions package has no local test infrastructure.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Stripe CLI webhook replay plus a Klaviyo sandbox check baked into the functions-package dev workflow, or a webhook-handler unit test with mocked Stripe/Klaviyo/Firebase clients.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Tester limits: 'Stripe/Klaviyo webhook flows cannot be E2E-verified via browser automation. Developer must run Stripe CLI replay or attach Stripe/Klaviyo dashboard screenshots in the Delivery comment.'
notes: 3 code-review rounds: 4 blocking findings round 1 (gate check, error handling), 1 blocking round 2 (per-learner try-catch isolation). Phase 4 Tester scenarios 0/4 executed - all required Klaviyo/Stripe dashboards. PR #1066 merged with no human review comments.

miss_root_cause: Symphony built and PR-ed a selective-unassign + assignment modal flow that another in-flight ticket (the 'due dates' work) had already delivered, so the human closed PR #931 unmerged and moved the ticket to Done.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Intent Analyst checking open Linear tickets and active branches for overlapping Figma-scope before Architect plans the change.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Intent: 'Before planning a Figma-driven UI ticket, list other open Linear issues that touch the same Figma frames or files; if overlap exists, ask the human to confirm scope split before building.'
notes: PR #931 CLOSED, not merged. Linear comment from Silas: 'This was done with due dates work, moving to done.' No .claude/ workpad/intent/qa artefacts present — ticket appears to have been worked w/o full Symphony flow, so retro signal is sparse.

miss_root_cause: Symphony upgraded a dependency in tts-service while main deleted that whole service (TTS moved to a separate repo), so the PR was closed unmerged as not needed.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A Phase 0 check on dependabot tickets confirming the target package/service is still maintained in this repo and not slated for removal or migration before running the full pipeline.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to Phase 0 for dependabot/dependency tickets: verify the affected package still exists and isn't being deleted/migrated on main (check recent main commits touching it) before investing the pipeline.
notes: Pipeline ran clean (intent/plan/dev/QA 3/3/review all green) but work was wasted: Linear is Done yet PR #1109 was CLOSED unmerged. Human: 'moved TTS to separate repo, so this is not needed.' Main commit e9434450 deleted tts-service, causing modify/delete conflicts on both touched files.

miss_root_cause: Symphony picked up a Dependabot ticket for python-multipart in packages/tts-service, but the whole TTS service was unused and removed via PR #1113, making the patch moot.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A pre-work check on whether the affected manifest/service is still in use or already slated for deletion.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md: before working a Dependabot ticket, check whether the affected manifest/service is unused or slated for removal and close as moot instead of patching.
notes: No Symphony PR, commits, or workflow artifacts. Vuln resolved out-of-band: human removed the unused TTS service (PR #1113) and closed Done with 'Not needed - TTS removed.' Original ask was a version bump; actual fix was service deletion.

miss_root_cause: Symphony filed a Dependabot ticket for packages/tts-service/uv.lock, but that service was already deleted from main in commit e943445 the same day.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Checking that the alert's manifest path still exists on main before filing or picking up the ticket.
proposed_workflow_change: Add a Dependabot intake guard: skip or auto-cancel a ticket when its target manifest path no longer exists on the default branch.
notes: No-op ticket. Agent correctly found the vulnerable manifest already removed (TTS service deleted in #1113), wrote a clean no-work-needed workpad, and escalated. Human confirmed 'not needed, TTS removed' and closed Done. No code, PR, or rework. Lesson is upstream intake noise, not execution.

miss_root_cause: Test matrix Expected assumed lint=0 and tests pass, but main already carried pre-existing lint and uuid ESM failures, so the Tester logged two false fails it had to triage against baseline.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Architect baselining lint and test status against origin/main before writing the matrix Expected.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md: for dependency/config-only changes, the Architect test matrix Expected must say 'no NEW failures vs origin/main', not 'exit 0', since the repo carries known lint/test debt.
notes: Clean protobufjs 7.5.9->8.6.5 security bump via pnpm overrides; PR #1115 merged, CI green, no rework, no human change request. Only friction: 2 Tester false-fails from an unbaselined matrix. VERIFY script skipped (macOS bash assoc-array incompat); individual typecheck/lint/test run instead.

Config-only Dependabot patch: npm override forces uuid>=11.1.1 (resolved 14.0.1) in functions/common. Audit 0 vulns, no new lint/typecheck failures vs baseline, code review approve, CI green, PR #1125 merged. No human comments, no rework. VERIFY's functions:lint masked by pre-existing eslint-not-on-PATH env gap (noted, not introduced).

miss_root_cause: Change merged with no rework loop and no human review comments; fix matched the original PostHog ask and all four InstantSearch roots.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Nothing needed to catch — but runtime reproduction of the uncaught error was never possible, so confidence rested on code inspection plus a correct react-instantsearch API.
proposed_workflow_change: Add a Playwright-compatible Algolia-failure mock (page.route intercept or injected client error) to the test harness so error-handler tickets can reproduce the symptom at runtime instead of falling back to code inspection.
notes: Tester ran 0/6 E2E: Playwright MCP can't block network (*algolia*), so AC1-AC4 passed by code inspection only; symptom never reproduced live. Hook test TDD-skipped (Jest React resolution) then back-filled post-merge in commit dffce56 to restore coverage (99.93%->100%). Big +402/-366 diff is mostly reformatting; real change is ~6-line hook + 4 integrations.

Clean one-line fix: swapped ErrorBoundary re-export for a local binding so React Router HMR tracks the module. No human comments, no rework, PR #1207 merged. Minor: workpad first blamed a package version mismatch, refined description said re-export; the re-export fix worked so no impact. AC2 (177-error count drop) deferred to post-deploy monitoring.

miss_root_cause: Two-line root-cause fix (await capture + .catch on IIFE) landed with test coverage, 100% coverage, and no human pushback.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Nothing needed; the flow worked as designed.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Common pitfalls: 'fire-and-forget async IIFEs (void (async () => {...})()) must have .catch(); several exist unguarded in packages/app/src (e.g. CoursesDialogAssign.tsx) — audit and fix in one pass.'
notes: Tester scenario 2 flagged an out-of-scope pre-existing void-async pattern in CoursesDialogAssign.tsx; resolved contextually (no dev re-run). Intent brief over-specified a posthog.ts wrapper the Architect correctly pruned to the minimal fix. Only CI comment was coverage bot (100%).

miss_root_cause: Optimization was already implemented; ticket was verification-only.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Nothing to catch — Architect correctly found the .select() projection already live across all 3 call sites before any code was written.
notes: Query Insights offender; investigation found .select() projection already in place at 3 call sites (5-6 fields vs ~25). No code change; only a .symphony-verified findings doc. Tester phase skipped (empty diff). Operator confirms read-op drop in BigQuery post-deploy.

miss_root_cause: Fix was a clean 3-line deletion of a server-only call wrongly placed in the universal root.tsx loader; root cause found in Phase 2 without needing the PostHog stack trace.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A seeded Firebase emulator would have let the Tester run the live scenarios instead of falling back to code inspection.
proposed_workflow_change: Pre-seed the workspace .data/ emulator export during Phase 0 setup so Tester scenarios that need auth are not blocked; and add a lint/test guard that fails when root.tsx imports from *.server.ts.
notes: Two AC items unmet but unflagged: no live reproduction (no PostHog access) and no regression test added (skip justified per TDD.md as pure deletion). Tester blocked by emulator stuck downloading Firestore JAR; verified by code inspection only. PostHog error-count drop stays unconfirmed until post-deploy.

Dev-only [react-router:hmr] noise leaking into PostHog. Fix suppresses the error at window.onerror + console.error before capture rather than fixing HMR itself; correct call for dev-only telemetry pollution. No human comments, single Dev->Review->Done path, CI green, review approved no blocking. Prod build scenario skipped by coordinator approval.

miss_root_cause: Shipped clean; no rework and no human review comments. One code-review round was an internal process gate (VERIFY line format), not a defect.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: N/A - no miss; the one-line default-limit fix matched intent and passed unit tests.
proposed_workflow_change: For query-insights tickets, seed course data in dev or route Tester to staging so the read-op reduction can be confirmed before merge instead of deferring the only functional check to post-deploy BigQuery.
notes: One-line fix: query.limit(limit ?? 50) in getCourseReviewsWithUser. Tester blocked on scenarios 1-2 (dev lacked course pages, /on-demand/* 404); BigQuery scenario deferred post-deploy. Core perf win unverified pre-merge, only unit test (13/13) covers behaviour. Code review flagged pre-existing N+1 (user fetch per review) - not filed as backlog.

Query-insights perf ticket. Root cause was volume: three loaders passed unbounded userIds to getLearnerStatsByUserIds. Fix reused the 60s Redis cache pattern from the -bulk endpoint on two remaining loaders. Query already had .select() projection + index, so caching, not limit(N), was correct. No human comments, CI green, 100% coverage on touched files. BigQuery read-drop verification deferred to post-deploy.

miss_root_cause: No miss; PostHog RangeError traced to unbounded recursion in 4 text-extraction functions and fixed with WeakSet cycle detection.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A shared cycle-safe traversal helper would have prevented the bug from being duplicated across 4 functions in the first place.
notes: Backend/logic fix, no UI. Root cause: circular Storyblok refs blew the call stack in 4 recursive extractors. Same WeakSet guard applied to each + 3 regression tests. CI pass, coverage 100%, code review approved (1 non-blocking pre-existing note), no rework, no human comments. Delivery comment concise.

Wrapped PostHog identify/logout in try-catch, logger.warn, 2 regression tests. Intent first guessed Firestore race per AGENT_MEMORY, but Explore agent corrected to PostHog SDK IndexedDB before build — self-fixed, no human needed. Scenario 2 verified via unit test only (browser repro blocked by CDN reload). Pre-existing app:lint debt noted, VERIFY exception per policy.

miss_root_cause: Shipped clean: PR #1220 merged, CI green, no tester rework and no human or reviewer pushback.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: N/A — no miss.
proposed_workflow_change: Make symphony/scripts/verify-changes.sh bash 3.2 compatible so the VERIFY gate runs natively on macOS instead of falling back to a manual gate.
notes: Symptom-suppression fix (unhandledrejection listener swallows PostHog IndexedDB 'Object Not Found' rejections); root cause is in PostHog's lib, so pragmatic and defensible. Two non-blocking process notes: Phase 2 architect sub-agents failed to write deliverables (parent recovered), and verify-changes.sh broke on bash 3.2 so VERIFY ran as a manual gate. AC4 (PostHog freeze) deferred to post-deploy.

miss_root_cause: Test matrix described IndexedDB-deletion scenarios that browser automation cannot perform, so QA fell back to unit tests instead of E2E.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Architect writing test-matrix steps only for behaviors the Tester's tools can actually drive would have flagged the untestable scenarios up front.
proposed_workflow_change: Architect should mark test-matrix rows that require capabilities the Tester lacks (e.g. deleting IndexedDB while connections are live) as unit-test-only, not E2E.
notes: Merged PR #1216, CI green, no human review or Linear comments, no state bounce-back. Fix wraps PostHog identify/reset and Firestore listeners to swallow 'Database deleted by request of the user' DOMException. Verified via unit tests only; E2E scenarios in matrix were not executable.

miss_root_cause: Architect ruled out unit tests as an API-swap with no branching, but AC2 (published filter) and AC4 (error fallback) could only be verified by unit test, so the Tester couldn't confirm them and the Developer added the tests mid-loop.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: If the Architect had mapped each AC to how it would be verified in the dev environment, it would have planned the AC2 and AC4 unit tests up front instead of after the Tester stalled.
proposed_workflow_change: Architect test matrix should tag each AC with its verification method and require a unit test for any AC that cannot be exercised end-to-end in the dev environment.
notes: Perf fix: swapped full-collection .get() for .count() with published==true filter in subscription.plans loader; PR #1222 merged clean, no human review comments. The one rework loop was about test coverage/verifiability, not a defect. AC3 (BigQuery read drop) deferred to post-deploy.

miss_root_cause: Main had already shipped TEA-4416 (#1215) for the same PostHog unhandled-rejection error; the overlap in usePosthog.ts surfaced only as a merge conflict, forcing the agent to reconcile logging conventions and tests.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A pre-work check for open or recently-merged PRs and sibling PostHog tickets touching the same file or error signature would have flagged the duplicate before development started.
proposed_workflow_change: Before starting a PostHog-sourced ticket, grep open/recent PRs and sibling PostHog error tickets for the same file and error signature to detect duplicate fixes.
notes: No Tester round-trip and no human review comments (rework/failures = 0), but not truly clean: merge conflict with TEA-4416 needed judgement to keep the async/await fix while adopting main's logger convention. Tester could not run E2E (chrome-devtools cannot capture console.error from async unhandledrejection handlers) so scenarios 1-4 passed via code review and were deferred to post-merge manual QA. CI green, coverage 100%.

miss_root_cause: The read reduction works by fetching only the first 50 learners with no pagination UI, so teams with more than 50 lose visibility of the rest — a behavioral trade-off self-declared 'acceptable' without human sign-off.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: A Tester run against seeded staging data with more than 50 learners would have shown the list silently truncated with no way to page to the rest.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md -> query-insights: 'when a read-reduction fix caps fetched rows (limit/single page), it must add pagination UI or flag the truncation as a behavioral change for human sign-off before merge.'
notes: Code Review caught B1 (client looped ALL pages, defeating the fix) and dev reworked to one page. But the merged fix caps the dashboard to 50 learners with no pagination UI. E2E deferred to staging; no human comments; CI green; PR #1221 merged. AC 'no behavioural regression' arguably not met.

miss_root_cause: Architect chose a .select() projection for latency, but both loaders spread the whole doc, so the projection had to cover all 24 fields — a no-op that cut neither reads nor payload, while the ticket's real goal was fewer read operations.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Checking that the loaders spread the full assignment object before committing to .select() would have shown the projection could shrink nothing, and re-reading intent (reduce reads; latency-only is out of scope) would have flagged the whole approach.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Query Insights: '.select() never lowers Firestore read-op billing and only cuts payload when callers read a strict subset of fields — if the loader spreads the whole doc it is a no-op; for a read-reduction goal use limit/scoping/caching instead.'
notes: Linear says Done but human closed PR #1223 unmerged: 'This doesn't help anything - the 24 fields are all the fields in this record.' Code review even forced expanding the projection to all 24 fields, guaranteeing the no-op. Tester skipped the one AC that mattered (reads drop) as post-deploy; team already knew .select() can't cut read billing.

miss_root_cause: No miss: requested items were already on main from an earlier nav rebuild, so no work was needed.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Nothing to catch — Phase 0 stale-work guard correctly recognized the scope was already delivered before any build started.
notes: No-op ticket. Redirects + Sync & Cache already wired in dashboard.tsx and superAdminNav.ts (restored by the TEA-4346 nav rebuild) and covered by a passing unit test. Phase 0 guard stopped, posted one explanatory agent comment, moved to Done. No PR, no rework, no tester run.

miss_root_cause: Type-only fix landed clean: 4 lines, QA 3/3, code review approve, PR merged, no human comments.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Nothing needed; the fix was surgical and correct.
notes: Reported TS2322 no longer reproduced on origin/main (codebase moved on), but the root mis-typing (let style: object) was still present, so the dev applied the proper fix (object->CSSProperties, null->undefined). VERIFY showed one fail from 81 pre-existing biome lint errors outside the diff (ticketed TEA-4439) - not a real miss.

miss_root_cause: Fast-path fix landed clean: root cause found, 6/6 test-matrix scenarios passed, code review approved with no blocking findings, CI green.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: N/A — no miss on this ticket; the recurring bug itself was caught only after 5 prior PRs missed the real root cause.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md -> PostHog: 'window.onunhandledrejection preventDefault() does NOT stop PostHog exception autocapture; drop noise via a before_send hook instead (load-order-independent).'
notes: Correctly diagnosed why 5 prior PRs failed: preventDefault() only sets defaultPrevented; PostHog's own onunhandledrejection still captures. before_send hook is the real fix. PR #1230 CI green, no human comments, no rework. PR shows state CLOSED not merged while Linear is Done — likely squash-merged elsewhere; commits still on branch.

miss_root_cause: Root cause and exact file were found in Phase 0, so the fast path skipped intent and architect and the change landed clean.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Nothing needed; the ticket shipped clean with 6/6 tester checks, an approved review, and green CI.
notes: Fast-path PostHog fix: before_send hook drops browser-extension 'Object Not Found' rejections; genuine errors kept. No human review comments (only coverage bot). Code review noted a harmless stale doc comment in filter-rejection.ts (IndexedDB vs extension origin), non-blocking. Only marked mem rule (firestore-loader-limit) not relevant to this frontend change.

Fast-path (Intent/Refine skipped, single concern). Dev in Progress -> In Review -> Done, no move-back. Tester 4 pass + 1 N/A, code review approved 0 blocking, CI green, PR #1228 merged. Fix: dev-only handler calls stopImmediatePropagation() behind import.meta.env.DEV so HMR rejections skip PostHog. VERIFY manual-pass = pre-existing whole-pkg lint/tsc debt (TEA-4440), reproduced identical on main; not a regression. Only human comments were bot posts.

miss_root_cause: Root cause was already fixed on main by TEA-4433 route-agnostic suppression; this ticket only pinned a regression test for the exact reported route.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Nothing needed — fast path was correct; the change shipped clean with no rework or human review comments.
proposed_workflow_change: Make verify-changes.sh app:lint diff-scoped (like typecheck) instead of whole-package biome, so pre-existing lint debt in untouched files stops tripping the VERIFY gate.
notes: Test-only change: route-pinned HMR regression test + jest.setup PromiseRejectionEvent polyfill (jsdom omits it, so entry.client tests silently didn't run). PR #1234 merged, 100% coverage, code review approved low-risk. VERIFY app:lint 'failed' but was pre-existing 36-error debt (identical on main), filed TEA-4440 — legit pre-existing-failure exception, not this diff.

miss_root_cause: No miss; scoped CURRENT reads cut by fetching the exact userId x courseId grid via getAll, parity locked by a deterministic read-count unit suite.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: N/A; the read-count and parity unit tests already gated merge as intended.
notes: Clean perf ticket. PR #1226 merged, checks green, code review approved no blocking, no human/reviewer comments (only coverage bot). Architect caught getAll-loses risk >30 courseIds and added a threshold fallback. UI parity rows 1-3 verified via unit parity test, not live UI (dev env unseeded, prod creds) per TEA-4399 lesson; no fabricated screenshot. app:lint skipped for pre-existing repo debt (TEA-4438 filed).

miss_root_cause: This run shipped clean, but the ticket only closed on attempt #22 — 21 earlier attempts patched Firestore when the error came from PostHog session recording (rrweb IndexedDB).
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Tracing which subsystem writes IndexedDB (PostHog rrweb, not Firestore, which uses memory cache) before patching would have skipped the 21 wrong-layer attempts.
proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md → Debug: 'An IndexedDB DOMException (e.g. "Database deleted by request of the user") comes from PostHog session recording (rrweb), not Firestore — the Firestore client uses the memory cache with no IndexedDB persistence; confirm the writing subsystem before patching.'
notes: Single forward path (Dev in Progress → In Review → Done), no move-back. QA 23/23 pass w/ regression proof (4 fail on origin/main source), code review approve/low-risk, no blocking, no human comments, PR #1232 merged. Only marked rule firestore-loader-limit not relevant (this was PostHog/IDB, not a Firestore loader). Two suppression defects fixed: DOMException not instanceof Error, and stopImmediatePropagation needed b/c PostHog autocapture ignores preventDefault.

miss_root_cause: No miss: delivery matched the intent brief, no rework, no human review comments, CI green.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: N/A — shipped clean on first pass.
notes: Cached the uncached /dashboard/courses loader (Redis, 10min, team-scoped key; merge admin user AFTER read to avoid cross-admin leak) instead of adding limit()/.select() — both would drop rows callers need (TEA-4424 trap, correctly rejected). Read-cut proven by unit test since Redis is null in dev; live BigQuery check is normal post-deploy step. app:lint skipped against a pre-existing 81-error baseline (backlog TEA-4439), transparent, zero new errors.

miss_root_cause: The one-line ask ('add these back for super admin') was vague, so a human had to post an exact spec (which menu items, and scope = all super-admins not just allowlist) before work could proceed.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: An Intent clarification pass that flagged the ambiguity and asked which menu items and which scope up front, instead of a first attempt that got kicked from In Review back to Dev.
proposed_workflow_change: Do not fast-path a vague one-line ask; require an Intent brief with concrete menu items and access scope before implementation starts.
notes: Clean minimal change (drop tools gate + sidebar filter), 100% coverage, tests green. Human merged PR #1235 early, pre-empting Tester browser E2E and code review (phases 4/4A/4.5). VERIFY app:lint fail was pre-existing repo debt, not this diff.

Query Insights fix. Replaced full published-reviews scan (~4,467 docs) in onReviewWrite with a Firestore aggregation query (count+average), cutting reads to ~5 per write. Chose aggregation over the ticket's suggested .select() projection, which does not lower read-op billing. PR #1240 merged, no reviewer comments, no rework. No .claude phase artefacts present.

miss_root_cause: Delivery added data-tour spotlight targets but never added logic to open the collapsed user menu, so the hidden Settings/Billing/Help items can't be highlighted at tour time.
what_would_have_caught_it_earlier: Running the actual tour flow end-to-end in the browser, which the Tester skipped because the dev server served stale code.
proposed_workflow_change: Block delivery when live E2E of the changed flow is skipped; the Tester must run the real interaction, not just confirm attributes render.
notes: Description stated tour 'needs to open and highlight the right-hand user menu'. Agent shipped only data-tour attrs (+Link data-* fix after 1 Tester bounce). Human fixed the open-menu behaviour and closed 11 days later. CI green, unit tests passed, but no live tour run.

proposed_workflow_change: Add to docs/AGENT_MEMORY.md -> Common pitfalls: 'Storyblok text-field values arrive as bare strings, not richtext objects; richText helpers must handle string input or they silently drop content.'
notes: Clean one-shot fix. Root cause: richTextToPlainText returned '' for non-object input, so EventFAQ blank-answer guard dropped every FAQ whose Storyblok answer was a plain string. Verified against live CDN, added unit test, PR #1245 merged with no rework and no human review changes. No local .claude artefacts present.
